buildSteps.label: Compare neighbors
buildSteps.description: Put negative weights on one side of the kernel and positive weights on the other. This subtracts one side from the other

buildSteps.label: Same values cancel out
buildSteps.description: In smooth regions, left and right pixels are similar, so subtracting them gives ~0 (no edge detected)

buildSteps.label: Different values light up
buildSteps.description: At a boundary, one side is dark and the other is bright. The difference is large, so the kernel output is large (edge!)

buildSteps.label: Direction matters
buildSteps.description: Negative-left / positive-right finds vertical edges. Negative-top / positive-bottom finds horizontal edges. Let's see which kernels do what...
